caption: The video appears to be a presentation or lecture on systems thinking and its application to rural community food systems. The presenter, Gene Bellinger, is seen in a small window on the right side of the screen, wearing headphones and speaking. The main content of the video consists of two different visual aids. Initially, a circular diagram is displayed, illustrating various interconnected concepts such as "awaken," "systems thinking," "model sharing," and "moments." This diagram highlights the relationships between different stages and elements of systems thinking. Later, the video transitions to a more complex network diagram related to the rural community food system. This diagram includes numerous nodes and connections, representing various aspects and interactions within the food system. Accompanying text provides context and instructions for contributing to the development of the model, emphasizing collaboration and community involvement. The overall focus of the video seems to be on understanding and improving rural community food systems through systems thinking methodologies.
